title: MATRIYA Failure-KB Ingestion Request
description: DESIGN artifact (contract) for the human-in-the-loop ingestion pipeline that turns a real source into a grounded Failure Case. This is a schema + gate contract, NOT a running ingestion: no fetch, no broad corpus. Its purpose is to make the pipeline's gates explicit and checkable — provenance confirmation, per-claim citation anchoring (the anti-invented-chemistry gate), evidence grading, MBM mapping, and dual-control sign-off.

submittedBy.description: a human submitter — the pipeline never auto-fetches or auto-submits.

submittedAt.description: ISO date supplied by the caller (no wall clock in the engine).

identifier.description: DOI, standard designation, patent no., or report id — the checkable handle.

license.description: copyright/licensing of the source — required before storing text.

editionOrDoiConfirmed.description: exact edition (standard) or DOI (paper) confirmed by a human.

proposedCase.description: the candidate Failure Case (validated against failure-case.schema.json at promotion). Every scientific claim must be backed by a confirmed citation anchor.

citationAnchors.description: the anti-invented-chemistry gate: each scientific claim traces to a verbatim span in the source, confirmed by a human. A claim with no confirmed anchor is rejected.

field.description: which proposedCase field this anchor supports (e.g. claimedMechanism, observedSymptoms, mappedMBM, antiConditions).

quote.description: verbatim span from the source supporting the claim.

confirmed.description: a human confirmed the anchor actually supports the claim.

reviewer2.description: dual control — must differ from reviewer1.
